questions.question: The time taken for an object in circular motion to complete one full revolution is called its:
questions.explanation: The period ($T$) is the time for one complete revolution. Frequency ($f$) is the number of revolutions per second.

questions.question: A ceiling fan rotates at a frequency of $4.0\text{ Hz}$. What is the period of one rotation?
A car travels around a circular track of radius $50\text{ m}$. It completes one lap in $31.4\text{ s}$. What is its linear speed? ($\pi = 3.14$)
Two objects move in circular paths with the same period. Object A is twice as far from the center as Object B. How does the speed of A compare to the speed of B?
options: A has half the speed of B
A has the same speed as B
A has twice the speed of B
A has four times the speed of B
questions.explanation: $v = 2\pi r / T$. With the same $T$, $v \propto r$. Doubling $r$ doubles $v$.

questions.question: The SI unit of frequency is:
questions.explanation: Frequency is measured in hertz (Hz), equivalent to cycles per second or $\text{s}^{-1}$.

questions.question: A washing machine drum spins at $1,200$ revolutions per minute (rpm). What is its frequency in Hz?
A point on a rotating disc moves at $12.56\text{ m/s}$ in a circle of radius $2.0\text{ m}$. What is its period? ($\pi = 3.14$)
A ball on a string moves at $15.7\text{ m/s}$ in a circle of radius $0.50\text{ m}$. What is the frequency of its motion? ($\pi = 3.14$)
A merry-go-round rotates with a frequency of $0.125\text{ Hz}$. How long does it take to complete one full rotation?
An object moves at constant speed in a circular path. If the radius is doubled while keeping the speed constant, what happens to the period?
questions.explanation: $T = 2\pi r / v$. With constant $v$, $T \propto r$. Doubling $r$ doubles $T$.

questions.question: A wheel of radius $0.30\text{ m}$ makes $50$ complete revolutions. What total distance does a point on the rim travel? ($\pi = 3.14$)
questions.explanation: Distance per revolution $= 2\pi r = 2 \times 3.14 \times 0.30 = 1.884\text{ m}$. Total $= 50 \times 1.884 = 94.2\text{ m}$.

questions.question: Two objects are in uniform circular motion. Object P completes $20$ revolutions in $4.0\text{ s}$. Object Q completes $30$ revolutions in $5.0\text{ s}$. Which object has the higher frequency?
questions.explanation: $f_P = 20/4.0 = 5.0\text{ Hz}$. $f_Q = 30/5.0 = 6.0\text{ Hz}$. Object Q has the higher frequency.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the fundamentals of circular motion!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the relationships between period, frequency, and linear speed.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on $v = 2\pi r / T$, $f = 1/T$, and unit conversions.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: period, frequency, linear speed in circular motion, and the formulas connecting them.
